01 / 03
Industrial Engineering—2024

Manufacturing Process Analysis

Comprehensive analysis of turning operations and production workflows to optimize manufacturing efficiency and resource utilization.

// Overview

This project undertook a rigorous examination of turning operations within a production environment, analyzing tool geometry, cutting parameters, and their combined influence on surface finish and dimensional accuracy. The goal was to identify bottlenecks and establish optimized parameter windows for sustained manufacturing efficiency.

// Methodology

Data was collected through direct observation of lathe operations, recording spindle speeds, feed rates, depth of cut, and tool wear intervals. Statistical process control (SPC) charts were constructed to monitor variation, and cause-and-effect diagrams were used to trace root causes of surface defects.

// Results

Optimized cutting parameters reduced average cycle time by 18% while improving surface finish Ra values from 3.2 µm to 1.6 µm. Tool life extended by an estimated 25% through adjusted feed-rate scheduling.

// Conclusion

The analysis demonstrated that disciplined parameter optimization—grounded in empirical data rather than heuristic convention—yields measurable gains in both quality and throughput. The findings have been compiled into a parameter reference guide for ongoing production use.
